author: lu_pinto
content:
Actually I've been thinking about it a little more. I feel the `reservoir` or the `spilled` vars are not needed at all. My reasoning is `TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT` is not a validation condition anymore after EIP-8037 - it's now a limit that will be tripped at runtime because we run out of gas at runtime due to the initial allocation. So mempool behaviour is no different - you need to run the transaction first to figure out whether it's going to OOG.

We could just have a single `gasLeft` pool but check `regular_gas_used` against `TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT` at runtime and OOG if it ever goes above. This would simplify the design greatly and can refill state used gas back to `gasLeft`. Just compute `regular_gas_remaining = min(gasLeft, TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT - total_regular_gas_used)` as clients are used to doing today (the decrement gas way)

author: garyrong
content: I guess the regular-left and state-left is still needed in case of unexceptional halt which drains the regular
